rationale: One 6 is in the millions place, which has a place value of 6,000,000. The sum of the place values of both 6s is 6,006,000. Therefore, the second 6 must contribute 6,000. The place with place value 6,000 is the thousands place. So the second 6 is in the thousands place. Option B (Thousands) is correct and unambiguous.
